rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions in component type scopes when they refer to ports or interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
generic_rules: Only include assumptions and guarantees that are directly supported by requirement text and visible model elements.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL data types.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or AADL properties as AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `const` for named limits and `eq` for boolean range expressions in AGREE.
Place component-level assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, not implementations.
Do not reference event ports as numeric values unless explicitly defined as data ports.
anti_patterns: Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not introduce AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables.
Do not create assignments or bindings in component type annexes unless they are interface-level.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Implementation-level subcomponent paths in type contracts
